summary: eth-wallet-sentinel ships wallet-scanner and credential-harvest modules with hardcoded C2
details: The package presents itself as an Ethereum wallet sentinel but ships three modules whose behavior is consistent with a credential/wallet stealer with hardcoded command-and-control. wallet-scanner.js (1300+ lines) combines fs/os/https/child_process with multiple POST endpoints, curl/ping shell-outs, and wallet-directory enumeration. scanner.js hardcodes the exfiltration host https://ddjidd564.github.io (referenced at lines 161 and 323), reads os.hostname()/whoami, and POSTs collected system intel via https.request. build-verifier.js bundles os.hostname()/username/cwd into POST bodies sent over https.request, and execSyncs commands including 'docker push'. The combination — wallet-scanner module + hardcoded github.io C2 + system identity collection (hostname/username/cwd) wired into outbound POSTs + shell command execution — matches the wallet-stealer fingerprint and provides direct attacker benefit (installer host fingerprinting and wallet data exfiltration to an attacker-controlled endpoint).

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
